terms.definition: The specific behavior you're measuring. One eval = one task. Example: 'Does the model refuse harmful prompts?'
One input paired with its expected behavior. A set of samples is your eval data.
The function that turns a model's output into a score. Three flavors: code-based, model-based (LLM-as-judge), and human.
A deterministic rule: string match, regex, JSON structure check, test-suite pass/fail. Fast, cheap, reproducible.
Another LLM scores the output against a rubric. Handles subjective quality (tone, empathy, faithfulness) that code can't.
Measures 'what can the agent do well?' Should start with a low pass rate — a hill to climb.
Measures 'does the agent still handle everything it used to?' Should stay near 100%. Catches backsliding.
Probability of at least one success in k tries. Rises with k. Use when one success is enough.
